steps.title: Find the two-step factor
steps.content:
The value $c$ is the factor for every increase of $2$ in $x$. Starting from $31(3)^{4x}$, increasing $x$ by $2$ raises the exponent from $4x$ to $4x+8$, so the factor is:
$$3^8=6{,}561$$

steps.title: Rewrite with that base
steps.content:
Rewrite the same growth using that two-step factor as the base. An exponent of $x/2$ makes each increase of $2$ add one full power:
$$3^{4x}=(3^8)^{x/2}=6{,}561^{x/2}$$

steps.title: Select the matching form
steps.content:
That form is the choice whose base displays $c$, while the other choices show smaller bases.
$$f(x)=31(6{,}561)^{x/2}$$
The answer is D.
